title: Fluffy Nutty Thai Coconut Khanom Krok
description: A nutty and fluffy Southern Thai dessert featuring coconut and lemon juice, made without eggs and simmered in a nimono style.
instructions: Combine coconut milk, rice flour, grated coconut, palm sugar, lemon juice, and salt in a large bowl.
Gradually add in the water and mix until a smooth batter forms.
Heat the peanut oil in a deep frying pan over medium heat.
Using a khanom krok mold or a mini muffin tin, pour the batter into each mold about 3/4 of the way full.
Place the mold in the frying pan and simmer for 5 minutes or until the bottoms are lightly browned.
Loosen the khanom krok with a spatula and flip them over to cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Remove the khanom krok from the mold and place them on a paper towel lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the fluffy nutty Thai coconut khanom krok warm, garnished with chopped peanuts.
